items.md: # *Indian Standard*
items.value: Indian Standard

items.md: # FIRE PROTECTION — SAFETY SIGNS
items.value: FIRE PROTECTION — SAFETY SIGNS

items.md: **1. Scope** — This standard covers the safety signs for use in the field of fire protection and fire fighting.
items.value: 1. Scope — This standard covers the safety signs for use in the field of fire protection and fire fighting.

items.md: **2. Safety Signs**
items.value: 2. Safety Signs

items.md: **2.1** The safety signs are presented in the tabular form based on:
items.value: 2.1 The safety signs are presented in the tabular form based on:

items.md:
* a) Means of giving warning of fire and manual controls ( *see* Table 1 ),
* b) Means of escape from fire ( *see* Table 2 ),
* c) Means of fire fighting ( *see* Table 3 ),
* d) Areas or materials of special fire risk ( *see* Table 4 ), and
* e) Supplementary signs ( *see* Table 5 ).
a) Means of giving warning of fire and manual controls ( *see* Table 1 ),
items.value: a) Means of giving warning of fire and manual controls ( see Table 1 ),

items.md: b) Means of escape from fire ( *see* Table 2 ),
items.value: b) Means of escape from fire ( see Table 2 ),

items.md: c) Means of fire fighting ( *see* Table 3 ),
items.value: c) Means of fire fighting ( see Table 3 ),

items.md: d) Areas or materials of special fire risk ( *see* Table 4 ), and
items.value: d) Areas or materials of special fire risk ( see Table 4 ), and

items.md: e) Supplementary signs ( *see* Table 5 ).
items.value: e) Supplementary signs ( see Table 5 ).

items.md: **2.2** The colour red, green and yellow, shall conform to Shade No. 536, 221 and 309 of IS : 5-1978 ' Colours for ready mixed paints and enamels ( *third revision* ) ' respectively. The paint shall conform to IS : 2932-1974 ' Specification for enamel, synthetic, exterior (a) undercoating, (b) finishing ( *first revision* ) '. The outermost dimensions of square ( side ), triangle ( altitude ), circle ( diameter ) shall be 125 mm and size of rectangle shall be 125 x 250 mm.
items.value: 2.2 The colour red, green and yellow, shall conform to Shade No. 536, 221 and 309 of IS : 5-1978 ' Colours for ready mixed paints and enamels ( third revision ) ' respectively. The paint shall conform to IS : 2932-1974 ' Specification for enamel, synthetic, exterior (a) undercoating, (b) finishing ( first revision ) '. The outermost dimensions of square ( side ), triangle ( altitude ), circle ( diameter ) shall be 125 mm and size of rectangle shall be 125 x 250 mm.

items.md: <naikassociates110@gmail.com>
## TABLE 1 MEANS OF GIVING WARNING AND MANUAL CONTROLS
items.value: TABLE 1 MEANS OF GIVING WARNING AND MANUAL CONTROLS

items.md:
| Sl No. | Sign | Meaning | Shape and Colours | Comments on Use | | ------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | 1 | \[A square sign with a red background and a white symbol consisting of a large circle with a smaller solid circle in the center.] | Manual activating device | Square sign
Background: red
Symbol: white | To be used to indicate either a fire alarm call point or the manual control of a fire protection system (e.g. fixed fire extinguishing system) | | 2 | \[A square sign with a red background and a white symbol of a loudspeaker or alarm sounder.] | Alarm sounder | Square
Background: red
Symbol: white | May be used alone or in conjunction with sign No. 1 above if the fire alarm call point actuates an audible alarm immediately perceptible to the occupants | | 3 | \[A square sign with a red background and a white symbol of a telephone handset.] | Telephone to be used in emergency | Square
Background: red
Symbol: white | Sign to indicate, or to show the position of a telephone available for giving warning in case of emergency |
rows: [A square sign with a red background and a white symbol consisting of a large circle with a smaller solid circle in the center.]
To be used to indicate either a fire alarm call point or the manual control of a fire protection system (e.g. fixed fire extinguishing system)
[A square sign with a red background and a white symbol of a loudspeaker or alarm sounder.]
May be used alone or in conjunction with sign No. 1 above if the fire alarm call point actuates an audible alarm immediately perceptible to the occupants
[A square sign with a red background and a white symbol of a telephone handset.]
Telephone to be used in emergency
Sign to indicate, or to show the position of a telephone available for giving warning in case of emergency
items.html:
Sl No. Sign Meaning Shape and Colours Comments on Use
1 [A square sign with a red background and a white symbol consisting of a large circle with a smaller solid circle in the center.] Manual activating device Square sign
Background: red
Symbol: white		 To be used to indicate either a fire alarm call point or the manual control of a fire protection system (e.g. fixed fire extinguishing system)
2 [A square sign with a red background and a white symbol of a loudspeaker or alarm sounder.] Alarm sounder Square
Background: red
Symbol: white		 May be used alone or in conjunction with sign No. 1 above if the fire alarm call point actuates an audible alarm immediately perceptible to the occupants
3 [A square sign with a red background and a white symbol of a telephone handset.] Telephone to be used in emergency Square
Background: red
Symbol: white		 Sign to indicate, or to show the position of a telephone available for giving warning in case of emergency

items.md: <naikassociates110@gmail.com>
# TABLE 2 MEANS OF ESCAPE
items.value: TABLE 2 MEANS OF ESCAPE

items.md:
| Sl No. | Sign | Meaning | Shape and Colours | Comments on Use | | ------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | 1 | \[The image shows two square signs. Each depicts a white stylized figure of a person running through an open doorway against a green background. One figure is running to the right, and the other is running to the left.] | Emergency exit | Square
Background: green
Symbol: white | This sign may be used to indicate all exits which can be used in the event of an emergency. It shall be accompanied by an arrow (Sign No. 1 of Table 5) unless the door is immediately apparent. It may indicate to right or to left. | | 2 | \[The image shows a round sign with a red circular band and a diagonal cross bar over a black symbol of a crate or obstruction on a white background.] | Do not obstruct | Round sign
Background: white
Symbol: black
Circular band and cross bar: red | Sign to be used in situations where obstruction would present a particular danger (escape routes, emergency exits, access to fire-fighting equipment, etc) | | 3 | \[The image shows a square sign with a green background. It depicts a white rectangle (door) with a white arrow pointing to the right, indicating a sliding motion.] | Slide to open | Square
Background: green
Symbol: white | To be used in conjunction with sign No. 4 of this table on sliding emergency exit doors, where they are permitted. May be used in the inverse direction | | 4 | \[The image shows a square sign with a green background. It depicts a white 3D-style arrow pointing diagonally upwards and to the right, indicating a pushing motion.] | Push to open | Square
Background: green
Symbol: white | This sign is to be placed on a door to indicate the direction of opening |
rows: [The image shows two square signs. Each depicts a white stylized figure of a person running through an open doorway against a green background. One figure is running to the right, and the other is running to the left.]

This sign may be used to indicate all exits which can be used in the event of an emergency. It shall be accompanied by an arrow (Sign No. 1 of Table 5) unless the door is immediately apparent. It may indicate to right or to left.
[The image shows a round sign with a red circular band and a diagonal cross bar over a black symbol of a crate or obstruction on a white background.]

Round sign
Background: white
Symbol: black
Circular band and cross bar: red
Sign to be used in situations where obstruction would present a particular danger (escape routes, emergency exits, access to fire-fighting equipment, etc)
[The image shows a square sign with a green background. It depicts a white rectangle (door) with a white arrow pointing to the right, indicating a sliding motion.]
To be used in conjunction with sign No. 4 of this table on sliding emergency exit doors, where they are permitted. May be used in the inverse direction
[The image shows a square sign with a green background. It depicts a white 3D-style arrow pointing diagonally upwards and to the right, indicating a pushing motion.]
This sign is to be placed on a door to indicate the direction of opening

Sl No. Sign Meaning Shape and Colours Comments on Use
1 [The image shows two square signs. Each depicts a white stylized figure of a person running through an open doorway against a green background. One figure is running to the right, and the other is running to the left.] Emergency exit Square
Background: green
Symbol: white		 This sign may be used to indicate all exits which can be used in the event of an emergency. It shall be accompanied by an arrow (Sign No. 1 of Table 5) unless the door is immediately apparent. It may indicate to right or to left.
2 [The image shows a round sign with a red circular band and a diagonal cross bar over a black symbol of a crate or obstruction on a white background.] Do not obstruct Round sign
Background: white
Symbol: black
Circular band and cross bar: red		 Sign to be used in situations where obstruction would present a particular danger (escape routes, emergency exits, access to fire-fighting equipment, etc)
3 [The image shows a square sign with a green background. It depicts a white rectangle (door) with a white arrow pointing to the right, indicating a sliding motion.] Slide to open Square
Background: green
Symbol: white		 To be used in conjunction with sign No. 4 of this table on sliding emergency exit doors, where they are permitted. May be used in the inverse direction
4 [The image shows a square sign with a green background. It depicts a white 3D-style arrow pointing diagonally upwards and to the right, indicating a pushing motion.] Push to open Square
Background: green
Symbol: white		 This sign is to be placed on a door to indicate the direction of opening

Background: green
Symbol: white | This sign is to be placed on a door to indicate the direction of opening | | 6 | \[A hand punching through a glass panel, represented by a jagged starburst shape] | Break to obtain access | Square
Background: green
Symbol: white | The sign may be used:
a) where it is necessary to break a glass panel to obtain access to a key or to a means of opening
b) where it is necessary to break open a panel to create an exit. |

a) where it is necessary to break a glass panel to obtain access to a key or to a means of opening
b) where it is necessary to break open a panel to create an exit.

# TABLE 4 AREAS OR MATERIALS OF SPECIAL FIRE RISK
items.value: TABLE 4 AREAS OR MATERIALS OF SPECIAL FIRE RISK

items.md:
| Sl No. | Sign | Meaning | Shape and Colours | Comments on Use | | ------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | 1 | \[A yellow triangle with a black border containing a black symbol of a flame] | Danger of fire — Highly flammable materials | Triangular
Background: yellow
Symbol: black
Triangle: black | To indicate the presence of highly flammable materials | | 2 | \[A yellow triangle with a black border containing a black symbol of a flame over a circle (oxidizing agent symbol)] | Danger of fire — Oxidizing materials | Triangular
Background: yellow
Symbol: black
Triangle: black | | | 3 | \[A yellow triangle with a black border containing a black symbol of an explosion] | Danger of explosion
Explosive materials | Triangular
Backgroud: yellow
Symbol: black
Triangle: black | To be used to indicate the possible existence of an explosive atmosphere, flammable gas or explosives | | 4 | \[A white circle with a red border and a red diagonal slash over a black symbol of a bucket pouring water onto a fire] | Water as extinguishing agent prohibited | Round
Background: white
Symbol: black
Circular band and cross-bar: red | To be used in all cases when the use of water on a fire is inappropriate | | 5 | \[A white circle with a red border and a red diagonal slash over a black symbol of a lit cigarette] | Smoking prohibited | Round
Background: white
Symbol: black
Circular band and cross-bar: red | To be used in cases where smoking can cause danger of fire |

To be used only together with one of signs No. 1 to 3 of Table 1 and 1 to 4 of Table 3 to indicate the direction of location of fire fighting equipment or a warning device

Background: green
Symbol: white		 To be used only together with sign No. 1 of Table 2 to indicate the direction to an emergency exit
